summary: Tested HRMS Settings Module (6 tabs) end-to-end: Departments, Teams, Designations, Holidays, Shifts CRUD; single/bulk shift assignment; shift resolve; attendance recompute; RBAC; legacy endpoint backward-compat. Wrote 14 pytest cases - all 14 PASS after 2 bug fixes applied. Ran Playwright smoke on /settings UI with HR admin.

critical.issue: 500 Internal Server Error. _sync_shift_to_employee(employee_id, shift) was called with resolver output (keys: shift_id, name) from _apply_active_shifts_now, but the function accessed shift['id'] and shift['name'] -> KeyError. Any call to recompute with at least one active assignment would crash. FIXED in settings_module.py: accept both 'id'/'shift_id' and 'name'/'shift_name'.

ui_bugs.issue: Hardcoded /api/employees?limit=1000 -> 422 because backend caps limit at 500. Caused Assign Shifts tab fetchAll() Promise.all to reject -> employees, shifts, depts, teams, designations and assignments all stayed empty, and 'Failed to load assign shifts data' toast shown. FIXED by changing limit to 500.

features_tested.frontend_settings_page_6_tabs_visible: PASS (departments, teams, designations, holidays, shifts, assign)

action_items: Verify fix in settings_module.py _sync_shift_to_employee - now handles both db-shift (id/name) and resolver (shift_id/shift_name) dicts
Verify Settings.js line 1044 now uses limit=500 instead of 1000
Main agent: consider auditing other places that might call _sync_shift_to_employee with resolver dicts (line 876 in settings_delete_assignment is also safe now)
Consider restarting frontend via sudo supervisorctl restart frontend to clear HMR cache so toast no longer appears for any cached state
critical_code_review_comments: BUG (fixed): settings_module.py _sync_shift_to_employee expected shift['id'] but _apply_active_shifts_now passes resolver output with shift_id key -> any recompute hitting an active assignment crashed 500.
BUG (fixed): Settings.js AssignShiftsTab uses /api/employees?limit=1000 which exceeds backend Pydantic limit (le=500) -> 422 breaks the entire tab.
Consider: settings_module.py imports UploadFile/FastAPIFile (line 30) but they appear unused - dead import.
Observation: /api/holidays legacy endpoint returns dict wrapper {holidays, stats}, while /api/settings/holidays returns bare list - inconsistent shapes. Not a bug, but callers must know.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (6 tabs load, sidebar present, dialogs open, CRUD flows work; Assign Shifts tab now loads without 422)

test_credentials.employee: user/user (spartasolace1 credentials from problem statement are INVALID on this env)

seed_data_creation: Test data created with prefix Test_<uuid>_* and cleaned up in-test. Leftover Test_*_AttShift shifts from earlier backend self-tests were also deleted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Settings module backend is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_settings_module.py (14 tests, ~2 min runtime). Valid test creds: admin/pass123 (hr), sysadmin/pass123 (system_admin), user/user (employee). The problem-statement employee creds spartasolace1/spar@1230 return 401 and should be updated in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Two bugs were fixed by this testing agent: (1) _sync_shift_to_employee KeyError on recompute, (2) frontend limit=1000 exceeding backend cap of 500. For future attendance rule engine testing: the /api/settings/shifts/resolve endpoint correctly returns the 10:00 start / grace=0 / total_hours=9 config; the actual 10:01 LATE vs 10:00 PRESENT assertion should be tested via calculate_attendance_status directly (needs attendance record creation at specific punch times - not covered here).
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE #1 (backend recompute 500): settings_module.py line 230 calls await _sync_shift_to_employee(e['id'], shift) where `shift` comes from resolve_shift_for_employee which returns {'shift_id': ..., 'name': ..., 'start_time': ...}. The function body accessed shift['id'] (line 217) -> KeyError. FIX: use shift.get('id') or shift.get('shift_id'). REPRO: assign any shift to any employee, then POST /api/settings/attendance/recompute with that employee_id -> 500. ROOT CAUSE #2 (Assign Shifts tab empty): Settings.js AssignShiftsTab fetchAll() hits /api/employees?limit=1000 but backend uses Pydantic Query(..., le=500). The 422 rejects the Promise.all entirely, wiping all state in the tab. FIX: limit=500.
